title: Components
description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

__schema1.title: Schema Object
__schema1.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema2.title: Reference Object
__schema2.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema3.title: Reference
__schema3.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema7.title: summary
__schema7.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: description
__schema8.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema10.title: Type Array Schema
__schema10.description: OpenAPI 3.1 (JSON Schema) allows type to be an array of primitive types.

__schema11.title: title
__schema11.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema12.title: description
__schema12.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema13.title: default
__schema13.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema14.title: example
__schema14.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema17.title: enum
__schema17.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema19.title: const
__schema19.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema21.title: Discriminator
__schema21.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema22.title: propertyName
__schema22.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema23.title: mapping
__schema23.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema28.title: XML
__schema28.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema29.title: name
__schema29.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema30.title: namespace
__schema30.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema31.title: prefix
__schema31.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema32.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema32.title: attribute

__schema34.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema34.title: wrapped

__schema38.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema38.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema39.title: description
__schema39.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema40.title: url
__schema40.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema42.title: readOnly
__schema42.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema43.title: writeOnly
__schema43.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema44.title: deprecated
__schema44.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema48.title: String Object
__schema48.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema50.title: format
__schema50.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema51.title: Pattern
__schema51.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema58.title: contentMediaType
__schema58.description: Media type of the encoded string content.

__schema60.title: Number Object
__schema60.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema62.title: format
__schema62.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema63.title: multipleOf
__schema63.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema65.title: minimum
__schema65.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema67.title: maximum
__schema67.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema69.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema69.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema71.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema71.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema73.title: Integer Object
__schema73.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema75.title: format
__schema75.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema76.title: multipleOf
__schema76.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema78.title: minimum
__schema78.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema80.title: maximum
__schema80.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema82.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema82.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema84.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema84.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema86.title: Boolean Object
__schema86.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema88.title: Null Object
__schema88.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema90.title: Array Object
__schema90.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema94.title: prefixItems
__schema94.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema96.title: contains
__schema96.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema108.title: Object Object
__schema108.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema132.title: Composition Schema
__schema132.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema133.title: allOf
__schema133.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema135.title: anyOf
__schema135.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema137.title: oneOf
__schema137.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema139.title: not
__schema139.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema141.title: if
__schema141.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema145.title: else
__schema145.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema148.title: Response
__schema148.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema149.title: Response
__schema149.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema153.title: Header
__schema153.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema154.title: Header
__schema154.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema155.title: description
__schema155.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema156.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema156.title: required

__schema158.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema158.title: deprecated

__schema160.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema160.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema162.title: style
__schema162.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema177.title: externalValue
__schema177.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema185.title: MediaType
__schema185.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema193.title: Encoding
__schema193.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema194.title: contentType
__schema194.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema210.title: Link
__schema210.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema211.title: Link
__schema211.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema212.title: operationId
__schema212.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema213.title: operationRef
__schema213.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema214.title: parameters
__schema214.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema217.title: requestBody
__schema217.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema221.title: url
__schema221.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema222.title: description
__schema222.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema223.title: variables
__schema223.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema225.title: ServerVariable
__schema225.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema226.title: enum
__schema226.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema228.title: default
__schema228.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema229.title: description
__schema229.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema237.title: name
__schema237.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema239.title: description
__schema239.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema244.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema244.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema247.title: explode
__schema247.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema248.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema248.title: allowReserved

__schema252.title: examples
__schema252.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema254.title: content
__schema254.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema258.title: name
__schema258.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema260.title: description
__schema260.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema261.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema261.title: required

__schema265.title: explode
__schema265.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema268.title: examples
__schema268.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema270.title: content
__schema270.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema274.title: name
__schema274.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema276.title: description
__schema276.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema281.title: style
__schema281.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema282.title: explode
__schema282.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema285.title: examples
__schema285.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema287.title: content
__schema287.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema293.title: description
__schema293.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema298.title: style
__schema298.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema299.title: explode
__schema299.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema302.title: examples
__schema302.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema304.title: content
__schema304.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema322.title: name
__schema322.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema323.title: in
__schema323.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema324.title: description
__schema324.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema326.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema326.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema329.title: bearerFormat
__schema329.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema330.title: description
__schema330.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema339.title: flows
__schema339.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema341.title: OAuthFlow
__schema341.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema342.title: authorizationUrl
__schema342.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema343.title: tokenUrl
__schema343.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema344.title: refreshUrl
__schema344.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema345.title: scopes
__schema345.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema358.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema358.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

additionalProperties.title: Callback
additionalProperties.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema364.title: Callback
__schema364.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema366.title: PathItem
__schema366.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema373.title: Operation
__schema373.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema374.title: tags
__schema374.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema376.title: summary
__schema376.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema377.title: description
__schema377.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema378.title: operationId
__schema378.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema379.title: parameters
__schema379.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema387.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema388.title: security
__schema388.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema389.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema389.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.
